home.heroLead: Raw honey and handmade beehives from the mountains of Tajikistan.

about.label: Our Story
about.title: A family apiary in the mountains of Tajikistan
about.body: Pchelovod is a small family operation in the mountains of Tajikistan. We tend our hives through the short but intense alpine season — from the first thaw to the last meadow bloom. The honey is raw, unfiltered, and moves as slowly as the bees that make it.

honey.label: Our Honey
honey.title: From the mountain meadows
honey.lead: Each variety reflects a different altitude, flora, and moment in the season.
honey.v1desc: Multi-floral, collected from alpine meadows at 2,500 m. Deep amber with a complex, slightly resinous finish.
honey.v2desc: Pale gold, slow to crystallize. Delicate aroma and clean sweetness from the lowland acacia groves.
honey.v3desc: Rare. Harvested above 3,000 m during the brief July bloom. Intensely aromatic, limited quantity each year.

beehives.label: Beehives
beehives.title: Handmade for mountain conditions
beehives.body: Our wooden hives are built from local timber using traditional Tajik techniques passed down through three generations. Each box is sized for mountain colonies — compact enough to carry up steep trails, robust enough to withstand harsh winters.
beehives.detail3: Available to other apiaries on request

queens.label: Queen Bees
queens.title: Carnica Sklenar queens
queens.body: We breed and sell Carnica Sklenar queen bees — one of the most productive and gentle lines in European beekeeping. Adapted to mountain climates and known for calm temperament, low swarming tendency, and excellent winter hardiness.
queens.detail3: Available by order, spring and summer

contact.label: Contact
contact.title: Get in touch
contact.lead: We respond to every message personally. For urgent inquiries, reach us on Telegram.

footer.tagline: Raw honey and beehives from the mountains of Tajikistan.

form.name: Your name
form.error: Something went wrong. Please email us directly.

bees.label: The Colony
bees.title: Life inside the hive
bees.body: Deep inside an active Tajik mountain hive, thousands of workers move with quiet precision — drawing wax, curing nectar, sealing each cell. This is where raw mountain honey begins.
